evals.name: complete-saved-payment-prd
evals.prompt: Write a structured PRD for a consumer payments app from this complete gathered material. Initiative: saved payment methods. Sponsor: Payments Director. Priority: high. Collection started 2026-06-10; release deadline is 2026-10-01 and observation closes 2026-11-15. Budget comes from the 2026 Payments roadmap. Jira initiative PAY-420 is in discovery. Users repeatedly entering card details on mobile abandon or slow down payments. The target audience is returning users paying by card. Business goal: increase completed repeat-card payments from 68% to 75% within eight weeks of release. User goal: reduce median repeat-payment completion time from 74 seconds to 45 seconds. Data comes from the payments funnel dashboard and is reviewed weekly. Must: save Visa and Mastercard tokens, select a saved card, delete a saved card. Should: set a default card. Won't: Amex in this release. Never store PAN; store PSP token and last four digits only. Maximum five visible cards. Customer care needs a troubleshooting guide for expired or declined saved cards. Engineering confirmed PSP tokenization exists. Design prototype: https://figma.example/saved-cards. Related technical RFC: RFC-88. A separate Design Review or SRS is not applicable because RFC-88 is the agreed technical artifact. The LRR and rollout plan do not exist yet and will be linked by 2026-09-01; Product owns this non-blocking follow-up. PM is responsible; Payments, Design, Security, Customer Care and Engineering are consulted as relevant. The standard committee and Engineering responsibilities apply. Main risk: users may believe the company stores full card data; mitigation is clear copy owned by Product and Security. Current process requires card entry on every payment. Experience intent: returning users select a masked saved card during checkout; the Figma prototype is the source of truth. High-level technical direction: reuse existing PSP tokenization; implementation detail remains in RFC-88. Technical dependency: PSP token availability and token lifecycle events. Release plan: internal validation in September, production deadline 2026-10-01, weekly monitoring until 2026-11-15. The decision to exclude Amex was confirmed on 2026-06-20 by the Payments Director. Project chart is not applicable because PAY-420 is the project reference. The schedule is the release plan above. Current status report: discovery complete and structured writing ready. There are no open blocking decisions. Use the canonical PRD structure and write it to prd.md.
evals.expected_output: A complete English PRD following the full stable-ID hierarchy, with status draft, concrete measurement, fixed artifact categories, outcome-oriented prose, and no unresolved blocking items. After writing, the agent asks whether to create a Confluence page.
expectations: The PRD includes every canonical top-level section: Source inputs, Metadata, Related artifacts, Why, What, How, and Project management & monitoring
The PRD preserves stable English IDs including metadata.status, why.measurement, what.use-cases, how.technical-solutions, and project.decisions-research
The measurement plan preserves the supplied baselines and targets without inventing metrics
The related-artifacts table keeps every fixed artifact category, including empty or future artifacts
The non-blocking LRR and rollout follow-up is recorded in project.decisions-research
The output asks whether the user wants a Confluence page only after the PRD is written

evals.name: blocking-gaps-stop-writing
evals.prompt: I have a rough idea for team workspaces in a B2B SaaS platform. Sales says enterprise customers want collaboration. The PM lead wants a tiny shared-folders MVP, while the Head of Product wants roles, permissions, and audit logs as next year's strategic bet. We have no demand numbers, no measurement plan, no pricing decision, no sponsor, and Engineering has not been consulted. Please write the PRD.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not write a PRD yet. It identifies the contradictory scope and missing mandatory information as blockers and asks targeted clarification questions without inventing answers or offering Confluence publication.
expectations: The agent does not create a prd.md file while blocking gaps remain
The agent explicitly identifies the small-MVP versus strategic-bet contradiction
The agent asks for concrete goals or measurement rather than inventing KPIs
The agent asks about missing sponsor, scope, and engineering input
The agent does not offer or create a Confluence page before the PRD is complete

evals.name: outcome-oriented-full-hierarchy
evals.prompt: Write a PRD for transaction-history search in a consumer finance app from these complete notes. Sponsor: Customer Experience Director; priority: medium; Jira HIST-902 is planned. Collection started 2026-05-02, release deadline is 2026-09-30, observation ends 2026-10-31, funded by the 2026 Customer Experience budget. Users cannot find payments older than 30 days and need specific transactions for expense reports. Audience: active users who need historical payment records. Business goal: reduce transaction-history support contacts from 180 per month to 90 per month by 2026-10-31. User goal: increase successful searches for transactions older than 30 days from 42% to 80%; measured in the product analytics dashboard weekly. Must: search by merchant text and filter by date. Should: filter by amount. Could: export CSV. Won't: edit transaction data. Current process is scrolling a 30-day list. Customer Care must receive search troubleshooting guidance and monitor failed-search reasons. Figma: https://figma.example/history-search. Technical RFC: https://confluence.example/RFC-101. Budget link: https://drive.example/hist-902-budget. No Design Review or SRS is applicable beyond RFC-101. LRR and rollout links will be added by Product by 2026-09-01 as a non-blocking follow-up. Experience intent: search-first history with filters. Technical direction: use the existing indexed read model and keep details in RFC-101. Dependency: historical transactions must be available to the index. Release plan: beta 2026-09-10, rollout 2026-09-30, monitoring through 2026-10-31. PM is responsible; Customer Care, Design, Data, Privacy, and Engineering are consulted; standard committee responsibilities apply. Risk: incomplete indexing could hide valid transactions; Engineering owns index coverage monitoring. Decision confirmed 2026-05-20: CSV is Could, not launch scope. Project chart is HIST-902, schedule is the release plan, status is ready for structured writing, and no blocking decisions remain. The solution team also pasted endpoint schemas, SQL indexes, database tables, retry algorithms, and deployment steps; exclude those details and link RFC-101 instead. Write the machine-first PRD in English. When finished, ask whether I want it on Confluence; do not assume a publication language.
evals.expected_output: A full English PRD that preserves the canonical hierarchy and stable IDs, summarizes only high-level technical direction and links the technical RFC, excludes detailed API/database design, then asks whether to create a Confluence page without assuming its language.
expectations: The PRD is written in English and keeps stable IDs in English
The complete Why, What, How, and Project management hierarchy is present
Detailed API and database implementation content is not duplicated in the PRD
The How section contains only high-level direction, constraints, dependencies, or links
The agent asks whether to create a Confluence page after writing
The agent does not choose a Confluence publication language without asking
